Title: Kennen
Age: CurrentAt least 9996 years old Timeline BornEarlier than 9000 BN Edit

children.title: Ancient Times
children.textContent:
Unlike many of his kind, Kennen felt a dissonance within the vibrant chaos of his homeland, Bandle City. Seeking harmony and balance, Kennen ventured forth from the spirit realm millennia ago, drawn by an insatiable curiosity that led him to traverse the material realm.

His journey eventually brought him to the serene First Lands, where ancient wars that none now remember had scarred the landscape, leaving behind a people striving to rebuild amidst the echoes of conflict. Kennen found solace among the inhabitants of the First Lands, fascinated by their relentless pursuit of balance and tranquility. Over time, he became deeply woven into the fabric of First Lands, forsaking his ties to Bandle City as he embraced the mortal realm and its inhabitants.

children.title: Acceptance into the Kinkou
children.textContent:
As Kennen delved deeper into the affairs of the First Lands, which would become known as Ionia, he encountered malevolent entities from the spirit realm threatening the delicate equilibrium of the land. Alone, he valiantly battled these disturbances, but soon realized that mere combat was not enough to address the root cause of imbalance. It was then that he discovered the Kinkou, a fledgling order dedicated to preserving harmony between the realms.

Recognizing the need for a unifying force within the order, Kennen proposed himself as an arbiter, a bridge between the introspective Eye of Twilight and the warlike Fist of Shadow. With his affability and diplomacy, he became the Kinkou's first Heart of the Tempest, working alongside the Eye and the Fist to pass judgment and maintain equilibrium.

children.title: War of the False Kanmei
children.textContent:
In one ancient conflict, the First Lands were invaded by a host of malevolent beings known as the Darkin, fallen god-warriors who had ravaged most of Valoran and Shurima in their conquests. Kennen, alongside the Eye of Twilight Bhinan and the Fist of Shadow Lao, each hoped to preserve the balance against these invaders, but some time after Lao was killed by the bloodweaver Xolaani, the Kinkou accepted an offer to ally with a Darkin who had turned against his brethren: Zaahen. Lao's successor, Yunara, kept an eye on and fought alongside Zaahen for the next three years, until the Fallen Ones were sealed within their weapons. When the war had ended, Zaahen requested he be imprisoned as well for fear of his own nature, and Bhinan tasked Yunara to guard his glaive within the spirit realm for all time.

After Yunara and Zaahen were safely banished to the Temple of Twilight, Kennen and the other Kinkou rewrote the history of the Great Darkin War into a wholesome fable known as the Tale of the Devoted Shrine Maiden, which cast Yunara as a humble shrine maiden and Zaahen as her patron kanmei, whom she followed into exile after freeing the world from the armies of false gods. With this story, the people of Ionia would be allowed to forget the hardship of the past and continue their lives in peace for centuries more.

children.title: Noxian Invasion of Ionia
children.textContent:
In the tumultuous aftermath of the Noxian invasion, as Ionia teetered on the brink of collapse, the Kinkou found themselves torn apart by internal strife and external threats. Despite Kennen's efforts to guide the order through the chaos, the shadow of betrayal loomed large as Zed, a former acolyte, orchestrated a coup.

In the darkest hour of the Kinkou, Kennen stood steadfast, offering counsel to the new Eye of Twilight, Shen, as he grappled with the burdens of leadership. He urged the Fist of Shadow, Mayym, to recognize Akali, his protege, as a potential successor. Yet even his wisdom could not prevent Akali's eventual departure from the order, disillusioned by its perceived failure to confront Ionia's enemies.

children.title: Spirit Blossom Festival
children.textContent:
Some time after being ousted from Thanjuul, the Kinkou Order took up residence in the Temple of Koeshin to rebuild. The Noxians withdrew from Ionia following the rise of a Grand General, and barely a season after the invaders left, the whole of Ionia bloomed with spirit blossoms, allowing for the first Spirit Blossom festival in years.

During the festival, Kennen encountered the Demacian seneschal Xin Zhao, who had come seeking wisdom about a Wyldbloom which had sprouted in the royal gardens. Even though he had been born in Raikkon, Xin Zhao confessed he felt like an outsider in Ionia having been gone so long. Kennen offered Xin Zhao wisdom and also invited him toward the Kinkou temple, where the Seneschal planted his Wyldbloom sapling on the mountain.

As Kennen and Xin Zhao were meditating near the peak, they heard a noise of a demonic incursion into Koeshin. As Xin Zhao went to fight the evil spirits, Kennen left to inform Masters Shen and Mayym. After speaking with Shen, Kennen returned to find Xin Zhao had defeated the demonkin with the help of Yunara, who had left her post at the Temple of Twilight to fight the demons. In the distance, they heard the temple had been breached by an unknown invader.

children.title: Trials of Twilight
children.textContent:
As they were leaving for the Temple of Twilight, Xin Zhao experienced a dark vision of his home kingdom in ruins, and he demanded to know what he and the Kinkou were up against. As the Seneschal had helped defeat the demonkin, Kennen and Yunara decided Xin Zhao could be trusted, and they informed him the truth of what had transpired in Ionia during the Great Darkin War. Kennen and Yunara both believed the Darkin were behind the attack and that they intended to free Zaahen from his prison in the Temple of Twilight.

Although Kennen had been ready to lead all the Kinkou acolytes into the temple, they found it had already retreated back to the Spirit Realm. This meant only two people at most could enter at once, and they would have to undergo the Trials of Twilight. After learning this, Kennen assigned the acolytes to equip Xin Zhao with new armor and a repaired spear, and he opened the portal to the Spirit Realm so he and Yunara would be able to make it back safely.

children.title: Discharging the Raishai Acolytes
children.textContent:
Kennen receives an urgent plea for help from the acolytes of the Temple of Raishai, on the southern coast of Zhyun, whose temple has been corrupted by an unknown malevolent force. As he races through varied landscapes, Kennen reflects on the weight of this mission, especially since it involves saving his fellow Kinkou members.

Arriving at the temple, Kennen discovers that the corruption lies not in the physical structure but within the acolytes themselves. They have become vessels for dark entities, fueled by their anguish and despair over the Kinkou's past decisions of non-intervention during times of conflict. Despite their loyalty to the order, their unresolved emotions have allowed these malevolent forces to take hold.

In a poignant moment, Kennen confronts the acolytes, gently revealing the truth of their condition and offering them a path to healing by releasing them from their Kinkou duties. However, some of the acolytes resist, seeing this as a dishonorable act and challenging Kennen. Using his lightning powers, Kennen incapacitates those who resist, not with the intent to harm but to break through their defenses and help them understand the necessity of leaving the order for their own well-being. Through his actions, Kennen not only dispels the immediate threat but also offers the acolytes a chance to find peace and balance once more.

As the former acolytes depart, Kennen reflects on the bittersweet nature of their departure, knowing that it was necessary for their own good and the greater harmony of the world. Despite the sorrow of parting ways, Kennen is resolved to carry their story forward, honoring their sacrifice and ensuring that their struggles are not forgotten.

mainContent.title: Appearance
mainContent.textContent: Kennen is a cream furred yordle with light brown hair and deep blue eyes. Usually he dresses in a purple ninja outfit with golden details.

mainContent.title: Personality
mainContent.textContent:
Kennen is a paradoxical being, due to his nature as a Yordle. Simultaneously an ancient being and a unaging spirit, Kennen can shift from a wise master to excitable and childlike in an instant.

When he first arrived on the Mortal Plane, Kennen became fascinated with humans and how they managed to do so much in so little time. After joining the Kinkou Order, Kennen took the responsability of recording the history of the Kinkou, and serving as the mediator of conflicts between the Mortal and Spiritual Planes.

children.title: Kinkou Order
children.textContent: Kennen, Akali, and Shen are members of the Kinkou Order. Like Shen, his duty is to preserve balance in Ionia.

children.title: Akali
children.textContent: Kennen was one of Akali's mentors, teaching her shuriken techniques, and emphasizing speed and agility over strength. He convinced her mother Mayym, the previous Fist of Shadow, to choose her as the successor. Kennen and Akali assisted Irelia alongside Yasuo and Karma in her resistance in a seaside village against the Noxian army that includes Sion.

children.title: Zed
children.textContent: The Kinkou Order is at war with Zed and his Order of Shadow. Kennen is willing to forgive the Master of Shadows should he be willing to seek redemption.

children.title: Teemo
children.textContent: Kennen is a friend of Teemo.

mainContent.textContent:
